headline: Geopolitical Shockwave Rattles Overnight Markets as U.S.-Iran Conflict Deepens
body_html:
Wednesday's open arrives under a heavy geopolitical cloud after the United States announced the completion of a fresh round of strikes against Iran overnight, drawing retaliatory fire toward sites in Bahrain and Kuwait and sending four oil and gas tankers reversing course from the Strait of Hormuz. The dollar climbed to a near week-high on safe-haven flows, crude futures extended gains on supply-disruption fears, and gold wavered as investors positioned cautiously ahead of this afternoon's Federal Reserve minutes release.

Tuesday's session — the last completed trading day — already signaled stress beneath the surface. Breadth tilted decisively negative, with 242 names falling against only 150 advancing across our 396-stock universe. Semiconductors bore the sharpest losses, off more than five percent as a group, while Materials and Industrials each shed more than two percent. Energy and Healthcare managed slim gains, the lone defensive patches of green on an otherwise bruised tape.

At the open, traders will be watching crude benchmarks closely given the Hormuz chokepoint risk, monitoring any NATO statement out of Ankara where alliance leaders are convening, and parsing the Fed minutes for any shift in rate-cut signaling. Risk appetite looks fragile; headline risk from the Middle East corridor remains the dominant variable.

briefs.headline: Hormuz Passage Disrupted as Tankers Reverse Course
briefs.body: Four oil and gas tankers turned back from the Strait of Hormuz following vessel attacks in the region, raising immediate concerns about a key global energy chokepoint. The incident underscores how quickly escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities can translate into physical commodity-supply risk, with crude futures responding to the upside in overnight trading.

briefs.headline: NATO Convenes in Ankara as Ceasefire Hangs in the Balance
briefs.body: Alliance leaders gathered in the Turkish capital to assess a U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement that appears increasingly fragile following the latest exchange of strikes. The NATO secretary-general characterized the U.S. military action as essential, while Iranian retaliatory strikes on Gulf neighbors complicated prospects for a durable halt to hostilities.

briefs.headline: Fed Minutes Due Wednesday Afternoon — A Secondary Focus Amid War Premium
briefs.body: Investors will scrutinize this afternoon's Federal Reserve meeting minutes for any updated guidance on the rate trajectory, though the release arrives in an unusually high-noise geopolitical environment. Gold's indecisive overnight session reflected the tension between safe-haven demand and the possibility that persistent energy-driven inflation could delay easing.

briefs.headline: China Eases Fuel Export Restrictions for July
briefs.body: Beijing lifted fuel export curbs for the month of July, according to sources familiar with the matter, a move that could help offset some global supply tightness stemming from Middle East disruptions. The decision signals a more accommodative posture on energy trade at a moment when international crude markets are already under pressure.

briefs.headline: Semiconductors Paced Tuesday's Decline With a Five-Percent-Plus Drop
briefs.body: The semiconductor group led all sectors lower in Tuesday's session, falling more than five percent as a cohort. Vicor Corporation, Teradyne, Alpha and Omega Semiconductor, Semtech, and Allegro MicroSystems each shed between roughly nine and ten percent, pointing to sector-specific headwinds compounding the broader risk-off tone.

briefs.headline: Asian and Emerging Markets Slide on Oil Surge and Risk-Off Tone
briefs.body: Indian equities dropped as rising crude prices following the U.S. strikes on Iran weighed on the country's import-heavy economy and dampened investor risk appetite. South Korean authorities separately flagged heightened vigilance around stock market volatility, while regional currency and equity markets broadly reflected the defensive repositioning seen in overnight futures.
